plan.what: Normalize the remaining documentation text that still shows mojibake so the README and the instruction block generated by stepgate init read as clean English in consistent UTF-8, without changing the CLI behavior or the protocol rules we just aligned.
plan.why: The previous step aligned the meaning of the docs, but some published text still contains encoding artifacts such as corrupted punctuation. Cleaning that up now makes the repository and the generated agent instructions readable and polished while preserving the same rules about prose proposals and the read-only behavior of stepgate next without --suggest.
plan.how: Replace the corrupted punctuation and encoding artifacts in the affected documentation text with clean English UTF-8 equivalents, then review the recently updated passages so the wording stays natural and consistent across the README, the generated instruction block, and the checked-in agent instruction files.
plan.expected_result: The documentation shown in the repository and injected by stepgate init will read as clean English without mojibake, while still describing the same protocol rules about prose proposals and stepgate next.
plan.verification: Review the diff and run text searches confirming that the common mojibake sequences are gone from the affected files and that the prose-proposal rule and the stepgate next explanation are still present in English.

data.summary: normalized the docs and injected instruction text to ASCII punctuation so the English guidance renders cleanly across terminals while preserving the same protocol rules
